title: Review the failing Canon v4 smoke tests in /tmp/phase0-1c8023. Focus on why test-canon-v4-smoke.mjs fails on commit 1c8023c7. Inspect fugue-execute.mjs and the test fixtures under scripts/tests/fixtures. Do not edit files. Return concise findings with exact file/line refs and the minimal code/test c...
summary_text: Findings (root cause of current failures on `1c8023c7`): 1. High: test fixture injection is ignored in `fugue-execute`. - Test expects mocks via env: [`test-canon-v4-smoke.mjs:47`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-canon-v4-smoke.mjs:47), [`:48`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-canon-v4-smoke.mjs:48). - Runner hardcodes real scripts: [`fugue-execute.mjs:43`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:43), [`:44`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:44). - Result: smoke run hits real lane/vote path, not `scripts/tests/fixtures/*`, so phase flow diverges and blocks at planning. 2. High: subprocess infra errors are misclassified as orchestration success/blocked vote. - Bridge marks almost any exit as ok (`code !== 2`): [`fugue-execute.mjs:369`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:369), [`:384`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:384). - Vote ignores non-zero exit and falls back to `BLOCKED` parse default: [`fugue-execute.mjs:395`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:395), [`:396`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:396), [`:424`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:424). - This explains subtest 2 mismatch (`Security veto...` expected, got `Vote blocked at planning` at [`test-canon-v4-smoke.mjs:121`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-canon-v4-smoke.mjs:121)). 3. Medium: child process launcher is host-dependent. - Uses literal `"node"` for child commands: [`fugue-execute.mjs:36`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:36), [`:368`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:368), [`:382`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:382), [`:391`](file:///tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs:391). - In this failing run it produced `code=127` in persisted state (node wrapper path issue), then cascaded into finding #2. Minimal changes needed (no broad refactor): 1. In `fugue-execute.mjs`, make bridge/vote paths overridable: - `BRIDGE = process.env.FUGUE_EXECUTE_BRIDGE ? path.resolve(...) : ...` - `VOTE = process.env.FUGUE_EXECUTE_VOTE ? path.resolve(...) : ...` 2. Replace child command `"node"` with `process.execPath` for budget/bridge/vote spawns. 3. Tighten exit handling: - Bridge `ok` should only accept expected success codes (`0` and intended partial-success code if applicable), not arbitrary non-`2`. - In vote path, if `result.code !== 0`, throw infra error instead of mapping to policy `BLOCKED`. Optional test hardening: - After resume in smoke test, assert mock state includes at least one `phase: "vote"` fixture call, so env-injection regressions fail immediately.
